column3: clamp_int It verifies v the value to clamp. min the lower bound (inclusive). max the upper bound (inclusive).

column6: ;Verify that when the input value v is below the lower bound (v < min)
column7: the function returns the lower bound (min) instead of the original value. This validates the first branch and lower-limit clamping behavior.

column6: ;Validate boundary behavior at the lower limit (v == min). When the value equals the lower bound
column8: so the function must return the input value unchanged.

column6: ;Validate nominal pass-through for values strictly inside the range. Confirms the else path is taken and the input value is returned unmodified.
;Validate boundary behavior at the upper limit (v == max). Values equal to the upper bound are considered within range; the function returns the input value unchanged.
;Verify that when the input value v is above the upper bound (v > max)
column7: the function returns the upper bound (max). This validates the second branch and upper-limit clamping.
